OpenWorkVulHeading: Open Work Permit for Vulnerable Workers
OpenWorkVulPara: Every employer in Canada is entitled to a safe and healthy work environment. Those being exploited at their workplaces can acquire an open work permit for vulnerable workers.
BenifitsHeading: Benefits of Open Work Permit for Vulnerable Workers
BenifitsList1: Flexibility: Work with nearly any employer across Canada.
BenifitsList2: Remedy for Abusive Environments: Provides a way to flee an abusive work environment and seek another job.
BenifitsList3: No Application Fee: There is no fee for applying.
BenifitsList4: Temporary Permit: Allows enough time to secure alternative employment and apply for a new work permit.
EligibilityHeading: Eligibility for Open Work Permit for Vulnerable Workers
EligibilityList1: You are inside Canada and hold a specific employer's work permit, such as a Labour Market Impact Assessment (LMIA)-based work permit or under the Seasonal Agricultural Worker Program.
EligibilityList2: You are facing or at risk of job-related abuse in Canada.
HowIRCCSubHead1: Any behavior that scares, controls, or isolates you could be abuse.
HowIRCCSubHead2: Abuse can be physical, sexual, financial, or mental and can include:
HowIRCCList1: Physical abuse, including assault and forcible confinement.
HowIRCCList2: Sexual abuse, including sexual contact without consent.
HowIRCCList3: Psychological abuse, including threats and intimidation.
HowIRCCList4: Financial abuse, including fraud and extortion.
HowIRCCList5: Reprisals, such as disciplinary measures, demotions, dismissals, or threats of such actions.
TypesEvidenceHeading: You should also include as much evidence as you can with your application.
TypesEvidenceList1: Letter, statement, or report from an abuse support organization, medical doctor, or health care professional
TypesEvidenceList3: Copy of an official report to an enforcement agency (e.g., police or Canada Border Services Agency)
TypesEvidenceList4: Official complaint to a provincial government enforcement agency (e.g., employment standards branch)
TypesEvidenceList9: Photos showing injuries or working conditions
TypesEvidencePara: The list above includes examples of evidence. You may have other types of evidence.
ha1: Collect extensive documents to provide evidence to IRCC.
ha2: Draft a mandatory letter describing the abuse, along with a sworn statement by the applicant.
ha3: Apply to IRCC and wait for processing, usually within a week.
ha4: The IRCC officer may request additional documents or, in rare cases, interview the applicant.
ha5: Once approved, IRCC sends a work permit by mail, which is valid for 1 year.
StillNotSurePara1: If you have received a refusal for any of the reasons mentioned above, do not worry. With over a decade of experience, we specialize in previously refused cases. While we don't provide jobs for LMIA, we can certainly assist you if you have a job offer. We have obtained approvals for clients who had multiple previous refusals. q1: Who is considered a vulnerable worker?
qa1:
Workers who are facing abuse are called vulnerable workers because of their status which depends on the employment from the specific employer.

Abuse can be physical, sexual, financial, or mental and can include:

  • physical abuse, including assault and forcible confinement
  • sexual abuse, including sexual contact without consent
  • psychological abuse, including threats and intimidation
  • financial abuse, including fraud and extortion
  • reprisals- a disciplinary measure, a demotion, a dismissal, any threat to take any of the measures mentioned above
q2: For how long will I be approved for a work permit?
q3: Can I extend my vulnerable open work permit?
qa3: Unfortunately, no, but you can look for different options to get a new work permit.
